set.body:
Child of bd-01KXZQCRJQ02WXW3PRY5BTQY5K.

Goal: leave a typed provider boundary for future sparse direct solvers without widening Gale's shared-core capability claim prematurely.

Scope and acceptance:
- Specify separate symbolic analysis, numeric factorization, solve, diagnostics, and capability-discovery contracts.
- Make reusable sparsity analysis and changing numeric values first-class.
- Keep providers optional and JVM-scoped unless a portable implementation is independently justified.
- Define ordering/permutation ownership, workspace, failure, and thread/backend semantics.
- Exercise the seam with contract tests or a deterministic test provider.
- Record an explicit go/no-go evidence gate for any SuiteSparse, faer-inspired, or pure provider implementation.
- Do not advertise sparse direct LU, Cholesky, or QR as supported merely because the seam exists.
set.title: Specify an optional sparse direct-factorization provider seam
